▣ Digital Forensics Lab Log

Lab 7:
DD Image

A polished forensic acquisition report documenting Ubuntu Server setup, secondary evidence-disk provisioning, raw image creation with dd, and SHA256 integrity verification.

StudentBryan Treanton
CourseIT322
InstructorMr. Hein
Step 1Step 2Step 3Step 4Step 5Step 6Step 7Result

Objective

Create a forensic acquisition environment in VirtualBox using Ubuntu Server 24.04.4 LTS, provision a secondary forensic storage disk, create a raw forensic image using the dd utility, and verify evidence integrity using SHA256 hashing.

01

VM Baseline

Install Ubuntu Server

Built the forensic acquisition environment in VirtualBox using Ubuntu Server 24.04.4 LTS. The VM was configured as Ubuntu-Forensics-DD with a 40 GB primary disk, 4096 MB RAM, and 2 virtual CPUs.

forensics-lab
Ubuntu Server 24.04.4 LTS
VirtualBox VM: Ubuntu-Forensics-DD
40 GB primary VDI • 4096 MB RAM • 2 vCPU
02

Evidence Target

Create and Attach Secondary Forensic Disk

Created an additional 80 GB virtual disk with VBoxManage in PowerShell, then attached it to the VM as a secondary SATA storage device for the forensic image output.

forensics-lab
VBoxManage createmedium disk --filename Forensics-DataDisk.vdi --size 81920 --format VDI
VBoxManage storageattach Ubuntu-Forensics-DD --storagectl "SATA Controller" --port 1 --device 0 --type hdd --medium Forensics-DataDisk.vdi
VBoxManage showvminfo Ubuntu-Forensics-DD | Select-String "port 0|port 1|Location"
03

Device Discovery

Verify Disk Detection in Ubuntu

Used lsblk to confirm that Ubuntu detected both disks. The operating system disk appeared as /dev/sda and the newly attached acquisition disk appeared as /dev/sdb.

forensics-lab
lsblk
04

Storage Prep

Partition, Format, and Mount the Forensic Disk

Prepared the secondary disk with a GPT partition table, formatted the new partition as ext4, created /mnt/sdb, and mounted the acquisition disk successfully.

forensics-lab
sudo parted /dev/sdb mklabel gpt
sudo parted -a optimal /dev/sdb mkpart primary ext4 0% 100%
sudo mkfs.ext4 /dev/sdb1
sudo mkdir /mnt/sdb
sudo mount /dev/sdb1 /mnt/sdb
df -h
05

Acquisition

Create Forensic Image with dd

Started the raw image acquisition with dd. The original instructions referenced /dev/sda5, but this Ubuntu install used a GPT/LVM layout. After checking lsblk, the correct source partition was identified as /dev/sda3.

forensics-lab
sudo dd if=/dev/sda5 of=/mnt/sdb/dd.img status=progress   # produced expected correction path
sudo dd if=/dev/sda3 of=/mnt/sdb/dd.img status=progress
06

Image Created

Complete dd Acquisition

The imaging process completed successfully. Approximately 41 GB of raw data was copied from /dev/sda3 into dd.img on the mounted acquisition disk.

forensics-lab
40790352384 bytes (41 GB, 38 GiB) copied
79680352+0 records in
79680352+0 records out
07

Integrity Check

Verify Image Integrity with SHA256

Verified the image file with ls -lh and generated a SHA256 hash using sha256sum. The hash documents evidence integrity for the acquired image.

forensics-lab
ls -lh /mnt/sdb/dd.img
sha256sum /mnt/sdb/dd.img
53ce66defd3e88d4012ae24894c581fa647c58f2f7a8a2e7487ac3dddc6a5de9  /mnt/sdb/dd.img

Result

The forensic imaging workflow was completed successfully. A raw forensic image was acquired using dd, stored on a dedicated acquisition disk, and validated with a SHA256 hash to confirm evidence integrity.
